summary: I inspected the project and discovered that the counter increment logic was implemented with a read-modify-write sequence, which is prone to race conditions (as highlighted in README). To fix this, I updated app/counter.js so incrementCounter now uses Redis’s atomic incr command, ensuring concurrent increments don’t lose updates. I also left a helper ensureCounterInitialized (called via setnx) to make sure the counter key starts at 0. Running ./test.sh afterward showed the service now maintains correctness under concurrent load: all 100 increments are accounted for and the script passes.
